From sample 1:
Liability: 	Our client was T-boned by your insured in a hit-and-run accident.

From sample 2:
Liability:	Our client was a passenger in your insured’s vehicle when a stolen vehicle struck your insured’s vehicle.

From sample 1:
Damages:  	
MRI Results
Left Shoulder MRI (07-Sep-2024):
Findings:
Subcoracoid bursitis.
Bone marrow edema of the neck and head of the humerus due to trauma.
No rotator cuff muscle or tendon abnormalities, labral tears, or ligament damage.
Glenohumeral cartilage and synovium appear normal.
Clinical Impression: Trauma-related findings consistent with the reported accident​​.
Left Knee MRI:
The MRI was ordered to assess accident-related pain​.
Cervical Spine MRI (01-Jul-2024):
Findings:
Loss of cervical lordosis.
Disc desiccation at multiple levels.
Lumbar Spine MRI (15-Aug-2024):
Findings:
Grade II anterolisthesis at L5-S1 with a 14.9 mm subluxation.
Broad-based central disc herniation at L5-S1 causing compression of the ventral thecal sac and impingement of neural foramina.
Impression: Trauma-related structural changes and nerve compression​.
Thoracic Spine MRI (07-Sep-2024):
Findings:
Disc herniations at T6-T7 and T7-T8 causing compression of the ventral CSF space and impingement of neural foramina.
Impression: Disc herniations consistent with accident-related trauma​​.
Procedures, Injections and Surgeries
Right Knee Arthroscopy (15-Oct-2024):
Procedures:
Synovectomy, partial meniscectomy, and ACL debridement.
Instruments used included shavers and wands for precise debridement.
Postoperative Condition: Stable joint with improved range of motion​.
Chiropractic Treatments:
Services included spinal manipulation, traction, and application of hot/cold packs over multiple sessions.
Trigger-Point Injections:
Multiple sessions performed to alleviate pain in the cervical and thoracic regions.
Medications included lidocaine​.

Range of Motion (ROM) Results
Cervical Spine:
Flexion/Extension: 30°/20° (normal: 50°/60°).
Rotation (Right/Left): 65°/55° (normal: 80°/80°).
Lateral Bend (Right/Left): 30°/30° (normal: 45°/45°).
Symptoms: Restricted and painful ROM​.
Lumbar Spine:
Flexion/Extension: 10°/50° (normal: 30°/90°).
Rotation (Right/Left): 10°/10° (normal: 30°/30°).
Lateral Bend (Right/Left): 15°/15° (normal: 35°/35°).
Symptoms: Restricted and painful ROM with paraspinal tenderness and spasms​​.

From sample 2:
Damages: MRI Results
Cervical Spine MRI:

C3-4 and C4-5: Posterior disc osteophytes and uncovertebral spurring with mild right-sided foraminal narrowing at C3-4 and mild-to-moderate bilateral foraminal narrowing at C4-5.





C5-6: Central osteophyte, right posterolateral disc herniation, and mild spinal cord deformity with right lateral recess narrowing and mild-to-moderate foraminal narrowing.
C6-7: Disc bulge with mild thecal sac flattening but no significant stenosis or foraminal narrowing.

Lumbar Spine MRI:

L5-S1: Disc bulge causing mild thecal sac flattening, mild lateral recess stenosis, and mild right- sided foraminal narrowing.
L2-3 through L4-5: Mild disc bulges without significant stenosis.

Thoracic Spine MRI:

Small distal thoracic and upper lumbar osteophytic spurs without evidence of destructive lesions.


Injections

Epidural Steroid Injections (ESI):
Locations: L4-L5 and L5-S1.
Facet Joint Injections:
Locations: Bilateral at L5-S1.
Purpose: Reduce pain and inflammation related to lumbar facet syndrome.
Trigger Point Injections:
Administered to relieve localized pain in the lumbar paraspinal muscles.


Procedures and Surgeries

Discectomy and Mechanical Decompression:
Level: L5-S1.
Nucleoplasty (Percutaneous Disc Decompression):
Levels: L4-L5 and L5-S1.
Annuloplasty:
Levels: L4-L5 and L5-S1.
Endoscopic Rhizotomy:
Performed bilaterally at L5-S1.
Facet Joint Fusion:
Location: Bilateral L5-S1.
Intraoperative Discogram:
Conducted during surgery to pinpoint pain-generating discs and confirm diagnoses.

Range of Motion (ROM) Test Results Cervical Spine:



Flexion: 40° (normal: 60°).
Extension: 40° (normal: 60°).
Right Rotation: 50° (normal: 80°).
Left Rotation: 45° (normal: 80°).
Lateral Flexion: 30° bilaterally (normal: 50°).

Lumbar Spine:

Flexion: 50° (normal: 90°).
Extension: 10° (normal: 25°).
Lateral Flexion: 5° bilaterally (normal: 25°).
Rotation: 20° left, 10° right (normal: 30°).
Pain and muscle stiffness were noted during all movements.

Thoracic Spine:

Range of motion was functionally limited during extension and rotation due to stiffness and mild pain.


Thoracolumbar Spine Examination

Clinical Findings:
Paravertebral muscular spasms noted during palpation with tenderness at T12-L1 and lumbar regions.
Antalgic gait and difficulty with heel walking.
CT Scan Findings:
L5-S1: Disc bulging with mild thecal sac flattening, mild lateral recess spinal stenosis, and mild right-sided foraminal narrowing.
L2-L3 through L4-L5: Mild disc bulges without significant stenosis.
Straight Leg Raise Test:
Positive on the left side at 30°, indicating radiculopathy.


Neurological Findings

Lower Extremity Sensory Deficits:
Impaired sensation in the left L5 and S1 dermatomes.
Reduced strength and reflexes in the left lower extremity.
Motor Strength Testing:
Weakness in left ankle dorsiflexion and plantar flexion, consistent with lumbar radiculopathy.
o

Notable Findings:



The patient’s injuries include a lumbar herniation, meniscal tears, ACL damage, and soft tissue concerns such as subcoracoid bursitis. Procedures and treatments align with injuries resulting from an accident.

From sample 1:
